query: What is the SI unit of acceleration?
preview: a = (v − u) / t where v is final velocity, u is initial velocity, t is time in seconds.

query: State Newton's first law of motion
preview: Newton's first law: every object remains in its state of rest or uniform motion unless an external unbalanced force acts on it.

query: What is the formula for acceleration?
preview: a = (v − u) / t where v is final velocity, u is initial velocity, t is time in seconds.

query: Define momentum and give its SI unit
preview: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity of an object. p = mv. The SI unit of momentum is kg·m/s.

query: Straight line on distance-time graph indicates?
preview: A straight line on a distance-time graph indicates that the object is in uniform motion.
The area enclosed between the velocity-time graph and the time axis gives the displacement of the object.
Newton's third law: for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction. The forces act on different objects.
Speed is the distance travelled per unit time (scalar). Velocity is the displacement per unit time (vector) with direction.
8.1 INTRODUCTION An object is said to be in motion when it moves from one place to another. A distance-time graph for uniform motion is a straight line.

query: How does Newton second law connect to momentum?
preview: Newton's second law: F = ma. The force F is measured in Newtons, m in kilograms, a in m/s².
